{"query":"Temu US local fulfillment model US-based sellers marketplace only transition details announced 2025","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1978,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://rethink.industries/articles/temu-opened-its-marketplace-to-u-s-sellers-a-year-ago-heres-how-3-brands-have-scaled/","title":"Temu Opened Its Marketplace to U.S. Sellers a Year Ago–Here’s How 3 Brands Have Scaled - RETHINK Retail","content":"Shop Succulents, a California-based retailer founded in 2012, built its business shipping live plants nationwide long before houseplants became a mainstream e-commerce category.
“The potential for scaling is enormous,” Janik-De Gennaro said.
Local Seller Program, Grumpy Butcher became the platform’s first seller of frozen foods, effectively helping define the category."},{"url":"https://www.wral.com/temu-says-it-s-only-shipping-from-the-us-that-doesn-t-mean-the-products-are-made-here/21990128/","title":"Temu says it’s only shipping from the US. That doesn’t mean the products are made here","content":"New York (CNN) — Hours after a key US tariff exemption expired on Friday, Chinese e-commerce site Temu announced it was overhauling its shipping model, sending out all American sales via US-based sellers.
And while US President Donald Trump says that he wants his new tariffs to help bring manufacturing back to the US, the move by Temu shows how major companies could evade that demand and try other tactics.
Sites like Temu and Shein have been building up their US warehouse portfolio for years to lower shipping times.
Shein began stockpiling goods, bulk shipping to US warehouses as early as last year, Chris Tang, a professor of global supply chain management at the University of California, Los Angeles, previously told CNN.
“Temu’s pricing for US consumers remains unchanged as the platform transitions to a local fulfillment model,” the company said in a statement.
Temu says on its website there are no import charges or extra costs on delivery for items purchased from local warehouses."},{"url":"https://cedcommerce.com/blog/how-to-sell-on-temu-in-2025-complete-guide-for-merchants/","title":"How to Sell on Temu in 2025: A Guide for Merchants","content":"When the US de minimis exemption ended in April 2025 and tariffs on Chinese imports spiked, Temu’s marketplace hit a wall.
“Is Temu only for Chinese factories?”
Sync Inventory Across Platforms: Use third-party connectors like CedCommerce to manage stock levels across Temu, Shopify, WooCommerce, and other marketplaces."},{"url":"https://finance.yahoo.com/news/temu-halts-shipments-china-us-201551026.html","title":"Temu halts shipments from China to US, shifts to selling from American warehouses","content":"Days after a key trade loophole closed, e-commerce platform Temu has overhauled its business model by halting shipments of Chinese-made goods to U.S. customers.
Instead of offering American consumers a plethora of discounted products from China, the company is now selling goods shipped from warehouses based in the U.S.
"All sales in the U.S. are now handled by locally based sellers, with orders fulfilled from within the country."
Temu has also "been actively recruiting U.S. sellers to join the platform" in order to help local merchants reach more customers and grow their businesses.
Temu and competitor Shein warned customers about potential price hikes in late April weeks after tariff wars began between the U.S. and other countries, most notably China."},{"url":"https://www.marketplacepulse.com/articles/temu-marketplace-inviting-us-sellers","title":"Temu Marketplace Is Inviting US Sellers - Marketplace Pulse","content":"According to reports in China, on March 15th, Temu will expand its marketplace to allow U.S. and, later, European sellers.
Unlike Temu’s existing Chinese sellers, they will not ship goods to Temu’s warehouses in China and instead handle fulfillment from their domestic warehouses.
Temu will also not manage pricing or marketing, thus effectively mimicking the Amazon, eBay, and Walmart marketplaces."}]}

Get PDF of the blog in your mail. When the US de minimis exemption ended in April 2025 and tariffs on Chinese imports spiked, **Temu’s marketplace** hit a wall. For months, the Chinese marketplace seemed like they’d given up on the US market entirely. Fast forward to Q3 2025: Temu is back among the top downloaded shopping apps in the US, operating with a leaner, smarter, and more diversified strategy. Local US sellers are now a core part of this pivot as Temu builds Western supply chains to stay competitive in a post-tariff trade environment. This blog is your guide for selling on Temu in 2025. * Become the #1 downloaded shopping app in both the US and UK. > No. While Temu initially relied heavily on Chinese suppliers, it’s now welcoming local businesses and individuals in the US, UK, and EU to build out its product catalog.","score":0.69199765,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/wilmingtoncalifornia/posts/temu-just-made-a-big-changestarting-may-2-2025-theyre-no-longer-shipping-stuff-s/1021195110118973","title":"Temu just made a big change. Starting May 2, 2025 ...","content":"## Wilmington Chronicles's Post. ### **Wilmington Chronicles**. Starting May 2, 2025, they’re no longer shipping stuff straight from China to the U.S. Because the U.S. government ended a rule that used to let packages under $800 skip taxes. Now, Temu would have to pay a huge fee—up to 145%—to send stuff from China. Temu is now trying to ship from sellers already in the U.S. instead. Temu’s trying to avoid those extra costs, but shopping on there might feel different now.
